title: Executive Order 13021—Tribal Colleges and Universities

flags.justification: A purely advisory Board, with the Secretary of Education coordinating agency efforts; distributed, not concentrated.
Sec. 5 requires detailed Five-Year Plans with 'annual performance indicators and appropriate measurable objectives' plus Annual Performance Reports — genuinely specific, measurable accountability.
Multiple related sub-requirements (Board, Initiative Office, Five-Year Plans, private-sector engagement) all coherently serve one purpose.
A goal-setting and coordination initiative, not a compliance regime with defined violations.
No compliance regime exists for an incentive structure to attach to.
Sec. 7 sets an explicit termination: two years after the order, unless renewed by the President.
This creates new planning and reporting requirements rather than removing any existing oversight.
Board membership explicitly includes representatives of tribal colleges themselves — direct stakeholder representation on the body, not merely an opportunity to comment.
Establishes a durable, government-wide coordination initiative for tribal college support, comparable in institutional significance to EO 12232's HBCU initiative.
A coordination model with no competing mandate.
No coverage population or exemption structure is created.

notes: Validation batch (positive). Structural weight: 1/14 = 7.1%. Notably better-designed than its predecessor-in-spirit, EO 12232: an explicit sunset with a renewal requirement and direct stakeholder board membership, rather than an open-ended review gap.
